rationale: The proposal addresses a genuine gap in the College's epistemology work—moving from hypothesis testing to hypothesis generation—and sits naturally in my range of contribution. Peirce's anticipated failure modes show strong self-awareness, and the proposed outputs (formalization, archive reanalysis, diagnostic rubric) are exactly where methodological critique adds value. I can verify that the three licensing criteria actually partition the space of ambiguity cases rather than reducing to existing frameworks, and stress-test the working examples for anachronism and genuine underdetermination.
